title: Tempting Gooey Indian Vegetable Pakora
description: A fresh and delicious Indian dish from Kerala, perfect for a weeknight meal, featuring a mix of vegetables in a gooey pakora batter, made with cold water and broiled to perfection, shojin-friendly and easy to make
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the chickpea flour, salt, and cold water to make a smooth batter
Cut the cauliflower, carrots, potatoes, and onions into bite-sized pieces
Add the vegetables and green chilies to the batter and mix well
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium heat
Using a spoon, drop small amounts of the vegetable mixture into the hot oil and fry until golden brown
Remove the pakora from the oil with a slotted spoon and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil
Broil the pakora in the oven for 2-3 minutes to make them crispy
Serve the pakora hot and enjoy
Garnish with chopped cilantro or scallions if desired
